# UC-17.1.81 · ISE Identity Risk Score Contribution to Splunk ES Risk-Based Alerting (RBA)

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Splunk ES gives every user and device a risk score that goes up when bad things happen. We measure how much ISE events contribute to those risk scores — to see if ISE is helping or just adding noise.*

---

## Description

Tracks how ISE-derived risk events contribute to Splunk ES RBA risk-object scores. Identifies users or endpoints whose ISE-only risk contribution exceeds a threshold — useful both for tuning RBA rules and for surfacing identity-anchored threats.

## Value

RBA aggregates many low-confidence signals into a high-confidence alert. Tracking ISE's contribution helps understand which ISE signals are pulling risk-objects above the alert threshold and which are background noise.

## Implementation

Schedule every 30 min. Requires ES Risk framework configured.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Trigger ISE-side: 10 failed auths + posture fail. ES Risk DM aggregates; UC-17.1.81 surfaces user.

### Negative scenario

No ISE risk activity. No fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
| from datamodel:"Risk"."All_Risk"
| where source IN ("ISE-AuthFail", "ISE-PostureFail", "ISE-TCNAC", "ISE-CII")
| stats sum(risk_score) as total_ise_risk by risk_object
| where total_ise_risk > 80
```

## Visualization

ISE-risk contribution by source; top risk objects from ISE-only events.

## Known False Positives

- Test accounts intentionally trigger many fails.
- Posture-grace-period users.

## References

- [Splunk ES — Risk-Based Alerting](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/ES/latest/User/RBA)
